title: Task Record Contract
description: Canonical JSON Schema for task records only. This contract defines the authoritative machine-readable source for per-task orchestration metadata and canonical delegation-brief rendering inputs. It intentionally excludes delegation-brief payloads, worker-result payloads, approval-policy rules, validator outputs, and runtime-gate decision artifacts.

task_id.description: Stable lexical task identifier that remains unique within a task set and serves as the deterministic tie-break key after declared_order.

task_name.description: Short deterministic task label used as the canonical task name for delegation-brief rendering.

operating_root.description: Explicit operating root for the task record, even when other tasks share the same default root.

project_context_reference.description: Ordered project-context references that remain authoritative task-record inputs for canonical delegation-brief rendering.

objective.description: Single task objective expressed as one authoritative string.

source_spec_reference.description: Ordered source-spec references that define the task and feed canonical delegation-brief rendering.

required_changes.description: Ordered required implementation changes for the task.

acceptance_criteria.description: Ordered observable acceptance criteria for the task.

out_of_scope.description: Ordered explicit boundaries that must remain outside this task record's scope.

local_evidence.description: Ordered local evidence paths or facts that remain part of the authoritative task context.

task_boundary_rules.description: Ordered task boundary rules, including stop conditions when completion would require broader package changes.

required_return_format.description: Ordered return-format requirements. This field remains an authoritative task-record input and should explicitly reference the canonical worker-result schema rather than ad hoc prose.

declared_order.description: Deterministic rollout-order value for task selection. Stable fallback ordering across tasks is declared_order then task_id.

files_in_scope.description: Ordered non-empty list of files that the task may edit.

validation_commands.description: Ordered non-empty list of validation commands for the task. The first command is the primary goal gate for lightweight build loops. Remaining commands are broader validation steps.

goal_validation.description: Explicit lightweight goal-loop metadata for deterministic primary-goal and safety-check execution.

approval_requirement.description: Authoritative per-task approval requirement declaration. This schema encodes only the value shape and does not define approval policy or runtime-gate behavior.

approval_state.description: Authoritative machine-readable approval state carried by the task record. This schema encodes only the legal state values and does not define transitions or dispatchability.

dependencies.description: Optional ordered dependency task identifiers. Values are expected to resolve against valid task_id entries in the surrounding task set.

dependency_unlock_score.description: Optional non-negative score used by higher-level ordering logic only when explicitly present.

risk_reduction_score.description: Optional non-negative score used by higher-level ordering logic only when explicitly present.

task_group.description: Optional stable grouping label for related task records.

notes.description: Optional ordered task-record notes that remain scoped to task metadata rather than inferred elsewhere.

non_empty_string_array.$comment: JSON arrays are ordered by definition; minItems preserves ordered non-empty lists for canonical task-record fields.

task_id.description: Lexical task identifier that supports stable references, deterministic ordering, and dependency links.

approval_requirement.description: Task-level approval requirement declaration only. This is not an approval-state transition model.

approval_state.description: Pure value-shape contract for approval state carried by task metadata.
